children.text: Which of the following is obligating for members of ICAO?

answers: ICAO shall approve the pricing of tickets on international airline connections
ICAO must be informed about differences from the standards in any of the Annexes to the convention
ICAO must be informed about new flight crew licenses and any suspended validity of such licenses
ICAO must be informed about changes in the national regulations
correctAnswer: ICAO must be informed about differences from the standards in any of the Annexes to the convention

children.text: The aircraft commander, when he has reasonable grounds to believe that a person has commited or is about to commit, on board the aircraft, an offense against penal law

answers: may require the assistance of passengers to restrain such person
may request such person to disembark
may not require or authorise the assistance of other crew members
may deliver such person to the competent authorities
correctAnswer: may deliver such person to the competent authorities

children.text: "Cabotage" refers to:

answers: crop spraying
a flight above territorial waters
a national air carrier
domestic air services
correctAnswer: domestic air services

children.text: The second freedom of the air is the:

answers: right to "cabotage" traffic, (trans-border traffic)
right to operate a commercial passenger flight with passengers on board between two states
right to land for a technical stop
right to overfly without landing
correctAnswer: right to land for a technical stop

children.text: The first freedom of the air is:

answers: The right to board passengers from the state where the aircraft is registered and to fly to an other state
The right to land for a technical stop
The right to overfly without landing
The opportunity to operate a commercial flight with passengers on board between two states
correctAnswer: The right to overfly without landing

children.text: The convention which deals with offences againts penal law, is

answers: the convention of Tokyo
the convention of Madrid
the convention of Rome
the convention of Warsaw
correctAnswer: the convention of Tokyo

children.text: The convention of Rome applies to damage:

answers: caused in the territory of a contracting state or in a ship or aircraft registered there in by an aircraft registered in the territory of another contraction state
only caused in the territory of a contracting state by an aircraft registered in the territory of another contracting state
caused in the territory of a contrating state by any aircraft regardless the registration
the above convention does not deal with this item
correctAnswer: caused in the territory of a contracting state or in a ship or aircraft registered there in by an aircraft registered in the territory of another contraction state

children.text: The international convention defining rules relative to the responsibilities of international air carriers for the carriage of passengers, baggage and freight is the:

answers: Tokyo Convention
Warsaw Convention
Montreal Convention
Hague Convention
correctAnswer: Warsaw Convention

children.text: A contracting state which continues to require the presentation of a cargo manifest shall, apart from the information indicated in the heading of the format of the cargo manifest, not require  more than the following item(s):

answers: The airway bill number
The airway bill number and the number of packages related to the air way bill number
The air waybill number; the number of packages related to each air waybill number and the nature of the goods
The airway bill number and the nature of the goods
correctAnswer: The air waybill number; the number of packages related to each air waybill number and the nature of the goods

children.text: Contracting states shall not require the authorized agent or pilot-in-command to deliver to the public authorities concerned, before departure of the aircraft, more than some copies of General Declaration, Cargo Manifest and stores list. The numbers of the copies are:

answers: 2 copies of General Declarations and Cargo Manifest and one copie of a simple stores list
2 of each
2 copies of General Declaration and of Cargo Manifest and of a stores list
3 of each
correctAnswer: 2 of each

children.text: In case of aircraft registered in other Contracting States, which are not engaged in schedule international services, and which are making flights across the territory of a Contracting State or stopping for non traffic purposes, such Contracting State shall accept the information contained in a flight plan as adequate advance notification. This information is to be received:

answers: at least 4 hours in advance of arrival
at least 2 hours in advance of arrival
at least 12 hours in advance of the expected ETA
at least 1 hour in advance of arrival
correctAnswer: at least 2 hours in advance of arrival

children.text: Which one of the statements is correct:

answers: contracting states shall accept an oral declaration of baggage only from crew
contracting states shall accept an oral declaration of baggage from passengers and crew
contracting states shall accept an oral declaration of baggage only from passengers
contracting states may not accept oral declaration of baggages
correctAnswer: contracting states shall accept an oral declaration of baggage from passengers and crew

children.text: The ICAO annex which deals with entry and departure of persons and their baggage in international flights is:

answers: annex 8
annex 9
annex 15
annex 6
correctAnswer: annex 9

children.text: When desinsecting is required by a Contracting State as a public health measure, the desinsecting is made when the aircraft is suitably equipped by means of an automatic dispersal or vapour while the aircraft is flying, but as far in advance as possible and:

answers: At least 30 minute prior to land
At least one hour prior to land
At least immediately before landing
At least when the aircraft enter that state airspace
correctAnswer: At least 30 minute prior to land

children.text: The obligation of a carrier to transport any person away from the territory of a Contracting State shall terminate from the moment such person has been definitely admitted in other Contracting State of destination.

answers: The obligation is for the Contracting State of the operator
The operator has no obligation
The obligation of the operator terminates as soon as the person leaves the aeroplane
The stated above is correct
correctAnswer: The stated above is correct

children.text: Unaccompanied baggage carried by air shall be cleared under the procedure applicable to:

answers: cargo and is covered by a traffic document
cargo but clearence documents provided by airlines shall be completed by the passenger prior to shipment
cargo but is free from any kind of declaration forms
accompanied baggage or under another simplified customs procedure distinct from that normally applicable to other cargo
correctAnswer: accompanied baggage or under another simplified customs procedure distinct from that normally applicable to other cargo

children.text: The documents for entry and departure of aircraft:

answers: has to be typewritten or produced by electronic data processing techniques
are accepted at the contracting state discretion
has to be typewritten
are accepted in handwritten block lettering in ink
correctAnswer: are accepted in handwritten block lettering in ink

children.text: When a person is found inadmissible and is returned to the operator for transport away from the territory of the state, the operator:

answers: shall not recover from such person any transportation costs arising from his (her) inadmissibility
is not responsible for the person inadmissible for entry in the receiving state
shall not be preclude from recovering from such person any transportation costs arising from his (her) inadmissibility
and the state of the operator are both responsible for the person inadmissible
correctAnswer: shall not be preclude from recovering from such person any transportation costs arising from his (her) inadmissibility

children.text: (CPL) The crew member certificate (CMC) shall be accepted by each Contracting State for identification purposes:

answers: Together with a valid flight crew license
Together with a valid passport
None of the above
Together with a visa if required
correctAnswer: None of the above

children.text: (CPL) When an airline crew member, in the exercise of her/his duties, travels to another Contracting State as a passenger in order to join an aircraft, she/he must carry for identification purposes:

answers: A CMc
A CMC together with a valid passport and visa if required
A temporary admission certificate issued by ICAO
A valid flight crew license
correctAnswer: A CMc

children.text: (CPL) What is the purpose of a CMC?

answers: To replace the crew member's licence
To permit access to the air side of an aerodrome for aircrew
To provide identification of aircrew
To allow flight crew to be exempt from customs, health and immigration facilities at an en-route aerodrome
correctAnswer: To provide identification of aircrew

children.text: The units responsable for promoting efficient organization of search and rescue service are:

answers: Alerting centre and rescue coordination centre
Flight information centre and rescue coordination centre
Area control centre, flight information centre and rescue coordination centre
Rescue coordination centre and rescue sub-centres
correctAnswer: Rescue coordination centre and rescue sub-centres

children.text: Three aircraft, (1), (2) and (3), arrive successively at ten minute intervals, overhead the scene of a recent aircraft accident. -aircraft (1) is unable to establish contact with the Search and Rescue Centre -aircraft (2) is able to contact the Search and Rescue Centre -aircraft (3) is a Search and Rescue helicopter The command of the situation is the responsibility of;

answers: (1), then by mutual consent (2) until the completion of operations
(1), then by mutual consent (2) and then (3)
(1), and then by mutual consent to (3)
(1) until the completion of operations
correctAnswer: (1), then by mutual consent (2) and then (3)

children.text: The color identification of the contents of droppable containers and packages containing survival equipment should take the form of coloured streamers according to the following code:

answers: Red for medical supplies and first aid equipment
Yellow for miscellaneous equipment
Blue for blankets and protective clothing
Black for food and water
correctAnswer: Red for medical supplies and first aid equipment

children.text: The color identification of the contents of droppable containers and packages containing survival equipment should take the form of coloured streamers according to the following code:

answers: Black for food and water
Blue for medical supplies and first aid equipment
Red for food and water
Yellow for blankets and protective clothing
correctAnswer: Yellow for blankets and protective clothing

children.text: The color identification of the contents of droppable containers and packages containing survival equipment should take the form of coloured streamers according to the following code:

answers: Black for food and water
Red for miscellaneous equipment
Yellow for medical supplies and first aid equipment
Blue for food and water
correctAnswer: Blue for food and water

children.text: The color identification of the contents of droppable containers and packages containing survival equipment should take the form of coloured streamers according to the following code:

answers: Yellow for medical supplies and first aid equipment
Red for food and water
Black for miscellaneous equipment
Blue for blankets and protective clothing
correctAnswer: Black for miscellaneous equipment

children.text: Using the ground - air visual code the letter(s) similar to the symbol meaning "REQUIRE ASSISTANCE" is (are):
Using the ground - air visual code the symbol meanning "we have found all personnel" is:
What is the meaning of the showed symbol in the ground air visual signal code for use by survivors?

answers: Require assistance
Drop emergency supplies at this point
Require medical assistance
Landing here impossible
correctAnswer: Require medical assistance

children.text: The ground - air visual code illustrated means:

answers: Proceding in the direction shown
Please indicate direction
Require medical assistance
Require assistance
correctAnswer: Require assistance

children.text: Using the ground - air visual signal code, the letter similar to the symbol meaning "REQUIRE MEDICAL ASSISTANCE" is:
What is the meanning of the showed symbol in the ground-air visual signal code for use by rescue units?

answers: operation completed
we have found all personnel
we have found only some personnel
we are returning to base
correctAnswer: operation completed

children.text: Which of the following is NOT an international distress frequency?

answers: 2430 KHz
correctAnswer: 2430 KHz

children.text: An aircraft is flying over a mountainous region in which a search is being carried out to find the survivors of an aircraft accident. The pilot sees a ground signal in the form of an "X". This indicates:

answers: "All occupants alive"
"Landing impossible"
"Need mechanical assistance"
"Need medical assistance"
correctAnswer: "Need medical assistance"

children.text: At night an aircraft observes a luminous signal requesting help. To indicate that he has received these ground signals, the pilot must:

answers: transmit, by luminous Morse signal, a series of the letter "R" using his navigational lights
switch his landing lights on and off twice or, if he is not so equipped, his navigation lights twice
make at least one complete turn over the group of people in difficulty
fly over the group of people in difficulty as low as possible
correctAnswer: switch his landing lights on and off twice or, if he is not so equipped, his navigation lights twice

children.text: For the transport of potentially disruptive passengers some supplementary safeguards are to be observed such as:

answers: the boarding has to be done at the state discretion
the boarding will be at the pilot in command discretion
boarding after to all other passengers
boarding prior to all passengers
correctAnswer: boarding prior to all passengers

children.text: The movement area of an airfield, the adjacent lands and buildings or the parts of them with controlled access is called:

answers: Security program
Manoeuvring area
Terminal
Aeronautical part
correctAnswer: Aeronautical part

children.text: Referring to the operational aspects in the unlawful seizure acts, it can be said:

answers: The contracting States will make provisions to ensure that an aircraft affected by an unlawful seizure act, which has landed in their territory, would be retained, unless its departure is justified to protect lives
The contracting States will not assist with navigation aids, air transit services, etc, to an aircraft affected by an unlawful seizure act
The contracting States will make provisions to ensure that an aircraft affected by an unlawful seizure act, which has landed in their territory, would be detained in all cases
The Annex 17 does not recognise the importance of consusltations between the State where an aircraft affected by an unlawful interference act has landed and the aircraft operator's State
correctAnswer: The contracting States will make provisions to ensure that an aircraft affected by an unlawful seizure act, which has landed in their territory, would be retained, unless its departure is justified to protect lives

children.text: Definition of "security, the ICAO Annex 17", is a combination of measures:

answers: and human and material resources intended to safeguard international civil aviation
and human and material resources intended to safeguard civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference
intended to safeguard international civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference
and human and material resources intended to safeguard international civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference
correctAnswer: and human and material resources intended to safeguard international civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference

children.text: (CPL) "The movement area of an airport, adjacent terrain and buildings or portions thereof, access to which is controlled" is the definition for:

answers: Operations side
Controlled airport area
Air side
Security area
correctAnswer: Air side

children.text: (CPL) The aim of aviation security is the following:

answers: Safeguard international civil aviation operations against acts of unlawful interference
Regulate the transportation of dangerous goods by air
Ensure that commercial flight operations are safe end secure
Ensure that adequate maintenance standards are applied by all operators
correctAnswer: Safeguard international civil aviation operations against acts of unlawful interference

children.text: (CPL) An airport security programme shall be established by each State for:

answers: Every airport that can accommodate jet aircraft
Airports that are perceived as being under increased threat of acts of unlawful interference
Any airport identified as having a poor safety record
Each airport serving international civil aviation
correctAnswer: Each airport serving international civil aviation

children.text: (CPL) Where a parking bay is not provided at an aerodrome for the use by aircraft that have been subject to unlawful interference, what is the minimum distance that parking bay is to be from other bays?
The national civil aviation security programme shall be etablished by:

answers: ECAC
ICAO
ICAO and other organications including the contracting state concerned
Each contracting state
correctAnswer: Each contracting state

children.text: Each contracting state shall designate an appropriate authority within its administration to be responsible for the development, implementation and maintenance of the national civil aviation security programme. The said appropriate authority:

answers: Shall be specified to ICAO and to ECAC
Shall be specified to ICAO
Should be specified to ICAO and to ECAC
Shall be specified to ICAO, ECAC and to other contracting states
correctAnswer: Shall be specified to ICAO

children.text: A State shall provide assistance to an aircraft subjected to an act of unlawful seizure. This assistance includes:

answers: provision of navigation aids, air traffic services, permission to land and catering for passengers
only permission to land
provision of navigation aids, air traffic services and permission to land
provision of navigation aids, air traffic services, permission to land and refuelling
correctAnswer: provision of navigation aids, air traffic services and permission to land

children.text: A State shall take adequate measures for the safety of passengers and crew of an aircraft which is subjected to an act of unlawful interference,

answers: until their journey can be continued
if is requested by an individual passenger
during a period of investigation
and arrange for them to return to their country of origin
correctAnswer: until their journey can be continued

children.text: (CPL) If an aircraft subjected to an act of unlawful seizure has landed on its territory, each Contracting State shall:

answers: Deliver food and water but turn down requests for fuel
Detain the aircraft on ground
a and c
Let the aircraft depart if it is necessary to protect human life
correctAnswer: a and c

children.text: Each contracting state shall establish measures to ensure that the aircraft operator is informed when passengers are obliged to travel because they have been the subject of judicial or administrative proceedings in order that appropriate security mesures can be taken

answers: the aircraft operator and the pilot in command are only to be informed when any passenger is the subject of judicial proceedings
these measures are of the discretion of the contracting state
the statement above question is incomplete. The pilot in command and the aircraft operator are to be informed
correct
correctAnswer: the statement above question is incomplete. The pilot in command and the aircraft operator are to be informed

children.text: When mixing or contact does take place between passengers subjected to security control and other persons not subjected to such control after the security screening points at airports serving internationnal civil aviation have been passed

answers: only the passengers are to be re screened
the passengers concerned and their cabin baggage shall be re screened before boarding an aircraft
only the passengers cabin baggage are to be re screened
the persons not subjected to security control shall be identified
correctAnswer: the passengers concerned and their cabin baggage shall be re screened before boarding an aircraft

children.text: Each member state should designate an appropriate authority with its administration to be responsible for the development implementation and maintenance of a national aviation security  programme. This programme should apply:

answers: only to passengers and aircrew in international civil transport flights
only to all international civil transport including aircraft engaged solely in the carriage of cargo
only to passangers and aircrew in international civil transport flights and domestic flights
to all international civil air transport including aircraft engaged solely in the carriage of cargo and yet to domestic flights at the discretion of each member state
correctAnswer: to all international civil air transport including aircraft engaged solely in the carriage of cargo and yet to domestic flights at the discretion of each member state

children.text: When a member state allows police officers, security staff, bodyguards or other agents of foreign states to carry weapons in their territory for the protection of aircraft in flight, permission for the carriage of weapons should be conditional upon:

answers: Agreement between the state of embarcation and the state of destination
Notification of the pilot in command of a decision to permit a weapon to be carried on board his aircraft only
Agreement between the state of embarcation and the airport of arrival

Prior notification by the state of embarcation to the foreign state in which the weapons will be carried on the airport of arrival and notification of the pilot in command of a decision to permit a weapon to be carried on board his aircraft
correctAnswer: Prior notification by the state of embarcation to the foreign state in which the weapons will be carried on the airport of arrival and notification of the pilot in command of a decision to permit a weapon to be carried on board his aircraft

children.text: Member states should introduce specific security measures for the air transport of the following groups of potentially disruptive passengers defined below:

answers: Deportees, inadmissible persons and persons in lawful custody
Deportees and inadmissible persons only
Deportees and persons in lawful custody only
None of the answers is correct
correctAnswer: Deportees, inadmissible persons and persons in lawful custody

children.text: Each State of ICAO Annex 17 shall ensure the establishment of a security programme,

answers: for every airline operating in the State
only for administrative staff of airport
That is common for all airports within State
At each airport
correctAnswer: At each airport

children.text: The ICAO Annex 17 comprise rules in order to establish security measures for passengers:

answers: Checked baggage, cargo and other goods, access control and airport design
and baggage
Cabin baggage, checked baggage, cargo and other goods, access control and airport design
checked baggage, cargo and other goods
correctAnswer: Cabin baggage, checked baggage, cargo and other goods, access control and airport design

children.text: Just before arriving on the apron, taxiing inadvertently on the grass, a wheel falls into a hole, which seriously damages the aircraft and obliges the crew to delay the departure.

answers: This is an irregularity in the operation, the crew must inform the operator of the delay caused by necessary repair
This is an incident and the pilot-in-command must report it to the airport authority within the next 48 hours
This is an accident and the crew must follow the procedure relevant to this case

Since no physical injury has been noticed and the flight is over, the actions to be taken are related only to insurance, to the repaire man, the operator and the persons in charge of the runway and taxiways
correctAnswer: This is an accident and the crew must follow the procedure relevant to this case

children.text: Who is responsible, under Annex 13 of the Chicago convention for the initation of an accident investigation?

answers: The government of the state in which the accident took place
Operators of the same aircraft type
The law enforcement authorities of the state in which the aircraft is registered
The aircraft manufacturer
correctAnswer: The government of the state in which the accident took place

children.text: Upon receipt of the modification and a request by the state of occurence for participation, the state of design and the state of manufacture shall in the case of an accident or serious incident inform the state of occurence of the name of its representative to be present at the investigation when the aircraft:

answers: Has a maximum mass over 2 250 kg
Has a maximum mass over 5 700 kg
Has a maximum mass over 27.000 kg
Has a maximum mass over 100.000 kg
correctAnswer: Has a maximum mass over 100.000 kg

children.text: The objective of the investigation of an accident or incident shall be the:

answers: prevention of accidents or incidents
prevention of accidents or incidents and to help the judges
prevention of accidents or incidents and to help the manufacturers in design
prevention of accidents or incidents and apportion blame or liability
correctAnswer: prevention of accidents or incidents

children.text: Which of the following,according to ICAO Annex 13, shall be entitled to appoint an accredited representative to participate in the investigation?

answers: any State which, on request, provides information, facilities or experts to the State conducting the investigation
all ICAO members States
State conducting the investigation, State of aircraft registry and operator's State
State conducting the investigation and State of design and manufacturing
correctAnswer: any State which, on request, provides information, facilities or experts to the State conducting the investigation

children.text: The accident investigation preliminary report shall be submitted to appropriate States and to the ICAO, in:

answers: The language of the investigating State
Any of the world's major languages
one of the working languages of ICAO
English
correctAnswer: one of the working languages of ICAO

children.text: The state of design shall ensure that, there exists a continuing structural integrity program to ensure the airworthiness of the aeroplane, which includes specific information concerning corrosion prevention and control, in respect of aeroplanes

answers: up to 5.700 kg maximum certificate take-off and landing mass
over 5.700 kg maximum certificate take-off and landing mass
over 5.700 kg maximum certificate take-off mass
up to 5.700 kg maximum certificate take-off mass
correctAnswer: over 5.700 kg maximum certificate take-off mass

children.text: The loading limitations shall include:

answers: all limiting mass, centres of gravity position and floor loadings
all limiting mass and centres of gravity
all limiting mass, mass distributions and centres of gravity
all limiting mass, centres of gravity position, mass distributions and floor loadings
correctAnswer: all limiting mass, centres of gravity position, mass distributions and floor loadings

children.text: Load factors has the following meaning:

answers: the ratio of a specified load to the mass of the aircraft, the former being expressed in terms of aerodynamic forces, inertia forces and ground reactions
the loads assumed to occur in the anticipated operating conditions
the ratio of a specified load to the weight of the aircraft, the former being expressed in terms of aerodynamic forces, inertia forces and ground reactions
the ratio of a specifed load to the mass or the aircraft the former being expressed in terms of aerodynamic and inertia forces
correctAnswer: the ratio of a specified load to the weight of the aircraft, the former being expressed in terms of aerodynamic forces, inertia forces and ground reactions

children.text: According to ICAO Annex 8, a certificate of airworthiness shall be renewed or shall remain valid subject to the:

answers: Requirements laid down by ICAO
Laws of the State of registry and operation
Laws of the State of registry
Laws of the State in which is operated
correctAnswer: Laws of the State of registry

children.text: The continuing airworthiness of an aircraft, according to ICAO Annex 8, shall be determined by:

answers: The operator's State
The State of registry
ICAO
A specific body
correctAnswer: The State of registry

children.text: An aircraft having sustained damage while on the territory of a Contracting State other than the State of Registry may fly without fare-paying passengers to an airport at which it can be restored to an airworthy condition if:

answers: The repair works may be done at an airport that is not more than 250 nm away
It receives permission from the manufacturer of the aircraft
It receives permission of the State of Registry
It receives permission from the Contracting State where the damage occurred
correctAnswer: It receives permission of the State of Registry

children.text: Who is competent to issue a Certificate of Airworthiness?

answers: The Contracting State which approves the aircraft on the basis of satisfactory evidence that the aircraft complies with appropriated airworthiness requirements
None of the above
Always the Contracting State in which the power plants have been designed, manufactured and approved
Only such qualified Contracting States specifically listed in Annex 7
correctAnswer: The Contracting State which approves the aircraft on the basis of satisfactory evidence that the aircraft complies with appropriated airworthiness requirements

children.text: The assigment of the common mark to a common mark registering authority will be made by:

answers: the International Civil Aviation Organisation
the state of registry and accepted by the International Telecommunication Union
the International Telecommunication Union
the state of registry
correctAnswer: the International Civil Aviation Organisation

children.text: The common mark shall be selected from the series of symbols included in the radio call signs allocated:

answers: to the State of registry by the International Telecommunication Union
to the state of registry by the International Civil Aviation Organisation
to the International Civil Aviation Organisation by the International Telecommunication Union
to state of the operator
correctAnswer: to the State of registry by the International Telecommunication Union

children.text: The registration mark shall be letters, numbers or a combination of letters and numbers and shall be that assigned by:

answers: the Internationnal Telecommunication Union
the International Civil Aviation Organisation
the state of registry or common mark registering authority
the state of registry only
correctAnswer: the state of registry or common mark registering authority

children.text: When letters are used for registration mark combinations shall not be used which might be confused with urgent signals for example

answers: LLL
TTT
RCC
FFF
correctAnswer: TTT

children.text: When letters are used for registration mark combinations shall not be used which might be confused with urgent signals for example

answers: DDD
RCC
PAN
LLL
correctAnswer: PAN

children.text: When letters are used for the registration mark combinations shall not be used which might be confused with urgent or distress signals for example

answers: RCC
LLL
DDD
XXX
correctAnswer: XXX

children.text: The certificate of registration shall:

answers: Be carried on board the aircraft at all times
Be visible to the passengers at all times
Be reproduced on the portion of the airline ticket that stays with the passenger
a) and c) are correct
correctAnswer: Be carried on board the aircraft at all times

children.text: When the first character of the registration mark is a letter:

answers: It may not be followed by a number
The registration mark must be completed by the national emblem
It shall be followed by a number
It shall be preceded by a hyphen
correctAnswer: It shall be preceded by a hyphen

children.text: The prescribed re-examination of a licence holder operating in an area distant from designated medical examination facilities may be deferred at the discretion of the licence authority, provided that such deferment shall only be made as an exception and shall not exceed:

answers: Two consecutive periods each of three month in the case a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in non-commercial operations
Two consecutive periods each of six month in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in non-commercial operations
A single period of three month in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in commercial operations
A single period of six month in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in non- commercial operations
correctAnswer: A single period of six month in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in non- commercial operations

children.text: When a contracting state renders valid a licence issued by another contracting state, the validity of the authorization:

answers: Depends on the regulations of the contracting state which renders valid the licence
Shall not extend beyond the period of validity of the licence
Shall not extend beyond one year for ATPL and CPL
Is only considered for PPL
correctAnswer: Shall not extend beyond the period of validity of the licence

children.text: You may act as a flight instructor to carry out flight instruction for the issue of a PPL

answers: With a CPL
With a theorical CPL examination plus flight instructor rating
With an ATPL
With a PPL plus flight instructor rating
correctAnswer: With a theorical CPL examination plus flight instructor rating
